creator: danfe@FreeBSD.org
text:
I don't think that bumping the version and repacking the distfile is a good idea.  It needlessly increases the entropy and makes users wonder why there are different versions on the Internet without really new upstream.  I appreciate your efforts to save the endangered ports, but repacking the distfile and minor documentation nits do not warrant a version bump.  Next time someone else makes the same mistake, and now we'd be having two 1.2 distfiles coming from different parties.

Give it a new home and stick to original distfile and version, apply necessary fixes; this is much more coherent and POLA-soothing way to go.

Also, please drop the formatting change of pkg-descr.  It is well fine as it is, it does not even cross 70-char boundary.  Our messages are not supposed to fit in just half of 80-char terminal width.  (I've already asked you this before I think.)
